---
title: "Animated GIF"
description: "Generate an animation of alignment behavior across relevant distances"
author: "Karel Dieussaert"
date: "2026-01-07"
categories: [Animation]
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
jupyter: python3
---
# Animated GIF

This example creates a GIF that visualizes alignment results over an increasing `relevant_distance` series.

## What you learn

1. How to process many distances in one run.
2. How to generate an animation with `animated_map(...)`.
3. How to detect stable zones where increased distance does not change geometry anymore.

## How to interpret the animation

- Early frames: small local corrections only.
- Mid frames: more boundary segments start snapping.
- Stable frames: geometry remains unchanged even when distance increases.

This helps choose practical distance ranges for production settings.

```{python}
#| label: fig-plots
#| fig-cap:
#|   - "Graphic"
{{< include ../../examples/example_process_animation.py >}}
```
